Dashboard Archive: 2026-05-22 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
Today's momentum-ranker data shows AXTI dominating the rankings with a composite score of 98.2 and rank #1, driven by extraordinary long-term returns (9887.94% over 1 year) that dwarf broad market indices like SPY and QQQ by roughly 9860 percentage points. However, this extreme outlier return comes with a notable structural concern: the ratio consistency score of 95.16 is strong but the 1-week-to-1-day ratio of only 0.84 suggests recent momentum has actually decelerated, even as the 1-month-to-1-week ratio jumped to 6.32, indicating volatile acceleration in intermediate timeframes. AXSM ranks second with a more modest 123.12% 1-year return and a composite score significantly lower, showing much steadier but less explosive momentum characteristics across all timeframes, with a superior bad_spy_score of 0.73 compared to AXTI's 5.21, meaning AXSM held up better on days when SPY declined. The data suggests a two-tier performance picture: AXTI represents extreme short-to-medium term momentum with execution risk in near-term consistency, while AXSM displays more sustainable, market-resilient gains.
